fileBaselines.content:
# Chat History (Copilot session backups)

This folder preserves the VS Code / GitHub Copilot **chat transcripts** for this
project so they are committed to Git and survive across machines and reinstalls.

## Why this exists

VS Code stores chat history **outside** the project, in your user profile:

```
%APPDATA%\Code\User\workspaceStorage\<hash>\chatSessions\*.jsonl
```

That folder is **not** part of the repo, so a normal `git push` does *not* back
up your conversations. If the profile is wiped, the history is lost even though
the code is safe on GitHub. This folder fixes that.

## How to use

Run the export script whenever you want to snapshot the latest chat history
(e.g. before pushing):

```powershell
.\chat-history\export-chat-history.ps1
git add chat-history
git commit -m "Snapshot chat history"
git push
```

The script auto-detects the correct `workspaceStorage` folder by matching this
repo's path (the `<hash>` differs per machine, so it is not hardcoded). It copies:

| Category              | What it is                                  |
|-----------------------|---------------------------------------------|
| `chatSessions`        | Full chat transcripts (`*.jsonl`)           |
| `chatEditingSessions` | Edit / checkpoint state                     |
| `debug-logs`          | Chronicle session logs                      |

Each machine's data is stored under its own `<hash>/` subfolder, and
`last-export.json` records the most recent export.

## Restoring on a new machine

These files are a **backup/record**. To view a transcript, open the `.jsonl`
under `<hash>/chatSessions/`. To make VS Code itself show them in the Chat
history list, copy the contents of `<hash>/chatSessions/` back into that
machine's `%APPDATA%\Code\User\workspaceStorage\<new-hash>\chatSessions\`
(find `<new-hash>` via the `workspace.json` whose `folder` points at this repo).

edits.text:
<#
.SYNOPSIS
    Exports VS Code Copilot chat history for THIS project into the repo so it
    syncs to GitHub alongside the code.

.DESCRIPTION
    VS Code stores chat transcripts outside the project, in your user profile:
        %APPDATA%\Code\User\workspaceStorage\<hash>\
    The <hash> is derived per-machine/per-path, so this script does NOT hardcode
    it. Instead it scans every workspaceStorage folder, reads its workspace.json,
    and matches the one whose "folder" URI points at this repo. It then copies:
        - chatSessions\*.jsonl        (the full chat transcripts)
        - chatEditingSessions\*       (edit/checkpoint state)
        - GitHub.copilot-chat\debug-logs\*  (Chronicle session logs)
    into chat-history\ under the repo, and writes a manifest.

    Run it whenever you want a snapshot (e.g. before `git push`). Existing files
    are overwritten with the latest versions; nothing in your profile is changed.

.NOTES
    Safe & read-only with respect to VS Code's own storage (copy only).
#>

[CmdletBinding()]
param(
    # Set of VS Code variants to search (stable + insiders).
    [string[]] $CodeVariants = @('Code', 'Code - Insiders')
)

$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'

# --- Resolve paths -----------------------------------------------------------
# This script lives in <repo>\chat-history\, so the repo root is its parent.
$ScriptDir = Split-Path -Parent $MyInvocation.MyCommand.Path
$RepoRoot  = Split-Path -Parent $ScriptDir
$DestRoot  = $ScriptDir   # export into chat-history\ itself

Write-Host "Repo root      : $RepoRoot"
Write-Host "Export target  : $DestRoot"

# Normalize the repo root to a comparable form (lowercased full path).
$RepoRootNorm = ([System.IO.Path]::GetFullPath($RepoRoot)).TrimEnd('\').ToLowerInvariant()

# --- Helper: decode a file:// URI to a Windows path --------------------------
function ConvertFrom-FileUri {
    param([string] $Uri)
    if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($Uri)) { return $null }
    try {
        $u = [System.Uri]$Uri
        if ($u.Scheme -ne 'file') { return $null }
        # LocalPath handles %20 etc. and gives a Windows path.
        return ([System.IO.Path]::GetFullPath($u.LocalPath)).TrimEnd('\').ToLowerInvariant()
    } catch {
        return $null
    }
}

# --- Locate the matching workspaceStorage folder ----------------------------
$appData = $env:APPDATA
if (-not $appData) { throw "APPDATA environment variable is not set." }

$matched = @()
foreach ($variant in $CodeVariants) {
    $wsStorage = Join-Path $appData "$variant\User\workspaceStorage"
    if (-not (Test-Path $wsStorage)) { continue }

    Get-ChildItem $wsStorage -Directory -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | ForEach-Object {
        $wsJson = Join-Path $_.FullName 'workspace.json'
        if (Test-Path $wsJson) {
            try {
                $folderUri = (Get-Content $wsJson -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json).folder
                $folderPath = ConvertFrom-FileUri $folderUri
                if ($folderPath -and $folderPath -eq $RepoRootNorm) {
                    $matched += [pscustomobject]@{
                        Variant = $variant
                        Hash    = $_.Name
                        Storage = $_.FullName
                    }
                }
            } catch { }
        }
    }
}

if ($matched.Count -eq 0) {
    Write-Warning "No VS Code workspaceStorage folder matched this repo path:"
    Write-Warning "  $RepoRootNorm"
    Write-Warning "Open this folder in VS Code at least once, then re-run."
    exit 1
}

Write-Host "Matched $($matched.Count) workspace storage folder(s)."

# --- Copy the chat data ------------------------------------------------------
$manifest = [System.Collections.Generic.List[object]]::new()

foreach ($m in $matched) {
    Write-Host "`n--- $($m.Variant)  [$($m.Hash)] ---"
    $destBase = Join-Path $DestRoot $m.Hash
    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $destBase | Out-Null

    $sources = @(
        @{ Name = 'chatSessions';        Path = Join-Path $m.Storage 'chatSessions' },
        @{ Name = 'chatEditingSessions'; Path = Join-Path $m.Storage 'chatEditingSessions' },
        @{ Name = 'debug-logs';          Path = Join-Path $m.Storage 'GitHub.copilot-chat\debug-logs' }
    )

    foreach ($src in $sources) {
        if (Test-Path $src.Path) {
            $destSub = Join-Path $destBase $src.Name
            New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $destSub | Out-Null
            Copy-Item -Path (Join-Path $src.Path '*') -Destination $destSub -Recurse -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
            $count = (Get-ChildItem $destSub -Recurse -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Measure-Object).Count
            Write-Host ("  {0,-20} {1,4} file(s)" -f $src.Name, $count)
            $manifest.Add([pscustomobject]@{
                variant   = $m.Variant
                hash      = $m.Hash
                category  = $src.Name
                fileCount = $count
            })
        } else {
            Write-Host ("  {0,-20} (none)" -f $src.Name)
        }
    }
}

# --- Write manifest ----------------------------------------------------------
$manifestPath = Join-Path $DestRoot 'last-export.json'
[pscustomobject]@{
    exportedAt = (Get-Date).ToString('o')
    repoRoot   = $RepoRoot
    machine    = $env:COMPUTERNAME
    user       = $env:USERNAME
    entries    = $manifest
} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5 | Set-Content -Path $manifestPath -Encoding UTF8

Write-Host "`nExport complete. Manifest: $manifestPath"
Write-Host "Now commit & push:  git add chat-history; git commit -m 'Snapshot chat history'; git push"

# Chat History (Copilot session backups)

This folder preserves the VS Code / GitHub Copilot **chat transcripts** for this
project so they are committed to Git and survive across machines and reinstalls.

## Why this exists

VS Code stores chat history **outside** the project, in your user profile:

```
%APPDATA%\Code\User\workspaceStorage\<hash>\chatSessions\*.jsonl
```

That folder is **not** part of the repo, so a normal `git push` does *not* back
up your conversations. If the profile is wiped, the history is lost even though
the code is safe on GitHub. This folder fixes that.

## How to use

Run the export script whenever you want to snapshot the latest chat history
(e.g. before pushing):

```powershell
.\chat-history\export-chat-history.ps1
git add chat-history
git commit -m "Snapshot chat history"
git push
```

The script auto-detects the correct `workspaceStorage` folder by matching this
repo's path (the `<hash>` differs per machine, so it is not hardcoded). It copies:

| Category              | What it is                                  |
|-----------------------|---------------------------------------------|
| `chatSessions`        | Full chat transcripts (`*.jsonl`)           |
| `chatEditingSessions` | Edit / checkpoint state                     |
| `debug-logs`          | Chronicle session logs                      |

Each machine's data is stored under its own `<hash>/` subfolder, and
`last-export.json` records the most recent export.

## Restoring on a new machine

These files are a **backup/record**. To view a transcript, open the `.jsonl`
under `<hash>/chatSessions/`. To make VS Code itself show them in the Chat
history list, copy the contents of `<hash>/chatSessions/` back into that
machine's `%APPDATA%\Code\User\workspaceStorage\<new-hash>\chatSessions\`
(find `<new-hash>` via the `workspace.json` whose `folder` points at this repo).

# VS Code writes URIs like  file:///c%3A/Users/.../Project  where the drive
# colon is percent-encoded. System.Uri.LocalPath mishandles that, so decode
# manually: strip the scheme, URL-unescape (%3A -> :, %20 -> space), then
# normalize slashes and any leading slash before the drive letter.
function ConvertFrom-FileUri {
    param([string] $Uri)
    if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($Uri)) { return $null }
    try {
        $p = $Uri -replace '^file:/+', ''          # drop "file:///" (any # of slashes)
        $p = [System.Uri]::UnescapeDataString($p)  # %3A -> :  ,  %20 -> space
        $p = $p -replace '/', '\'                   # forward -> back slashes
        $p = $p -replace '^\\+', ''                 # drop any leading backslash
        return ([System.IO.Path]::GetFullPath($p

#!/bin/sh
# Pre-commit hook: snapshot Copilot chat history into the repo so it is
# committed alongside the code. Runs the PowerShell export script, then stages
# the chat-history folder so it is part of THIS commit.
#
# Enable on a new machine with:
#   git config core.hooksPath .githooks
#
# To skip a single commit:  git commit --no-verify

repo_root=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)
script="$repo_root/chat-history/export-chat-history.ps1"

if [ ! -f "$script" ]; then
    echo "pre-commit: chat-history export script not found, skipping."
    exit 0
fi

# Find a PowerShell executable (Windows PowerShell or PowerShell Core).
ps_exe=""
for c in powershell.exe pwsh.exe pwsh; do
    if command -v "$c" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        ps_exe="$c"
        break
    fi
done

if [ -z "$ps_exe" ]; then
    echo "pre-commit: PowerShell not found on PATH, skipping chat-history export."
    exit 0
fi

echo "pre-commit: exporting Copilot chat history..."
"$ps_exe" -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File "$script"

# Stage whatever the export produced/updated.
git add "$repo_root/chat-history"

exit 0

Automatic snapshots (pre-commit hook)

A Git pre-commit hook in `.githooks/pre-commit` runs the export automatically on
every commit and stages the result, so chat history is captured without thinking
about it. The hook path is committed, but each clone must enable it once:

```powershell
git config core.hooksPath .githooks
```

To skip the export for a single commit, use `git commit --no-verify`.

## Manual snapshots

You can also run the export script directly whenever you want a snapshot:

```powershell
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File
